title: Hylocharis hispida
intro: Hylocharis hispida, also known as the Blue-throated Goldentail, is a species of hummingbird-pollinated orchid that is native to the tropical regions of Central America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Hylocharis hispida include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs.
